strategy: agentic chunking via Gemini (headline + summary + original text)

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT INTAKE FORM
Please fill in and save this form in Word, Google Docs or ODS.
The more information you provide, the better I will be able to assist you.
You can use either the imperial or metric system to fill in this form. Just
state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb, inch).

General
 Full legal name: Markos
 Street and house number:
 Postal code:
 City:
 Country:
 Age: 31
 Height: 5'11"
 Weight: 191 lbs (down from ~270 lbs)
 Body fat percentage (plus estimation method): ~20% (neck:waist
ratio)
 Years of training experience: ~1 year
Strength
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in
weight times reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
 Bench press: 1RM has been stuck at 195 lbs for a while now
 Squat (excluding body weight): 200 x 15 on leg press (don't feel
safe with heavy leg work)
 Chin-up (excluding body weight): Can do 2-3 bodyweight pullups on
a good day
 Overhead press: Don't perform this lift
headings: PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT INTAKE FORM
General
Strength

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

What are your fitness goals?
I've lost a substantial amount of weight since switching to a Vegan Diet
about a year ago. My weight loss has basically stopped, but I know I have
more weight to lose.
How often in a week would you be prepared to train for maximal
results?
I currently train 3x per week in the mornings (only time I can fit it around
work). Willing to bump that up to 4 days during the work week if that
helps, but I want a day off in there somewhere so I don't get beat up.
What is your occupation?
You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal trainer/manual
labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm, stress and
activity level.
Defense Attorney in criminal law. It's a high-stress job with many big
decisions & people's futures are literally on the line.
Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had:
I do have at least one herniated disc in my Lumbar Spine, I believe
between L5-S1. Nagging low back pain while sitting is a constant reminder
of this.
Do you insist on any special diet (e.g. ketogenic, vegan)?
Vegan Diet, yes. I made the switch partially for ethical reasons but since
I've already lost so much weight with it, I know it works.
Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please
list them:
Never have; never will.
headings: What are your fitness goals?
How often in a week would you be prepared to train for maximal
results?
What is your occupation?
Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had:
Do you insist on any special diet (e.g. ketogenic, vegan)?
Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please
list them:

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Lifestyle
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train?
(This includes when you're at work!) If so, please list them:
I spend all weekend reviewing cases & consulting with prospective clients,
which is why I can only train during the week. In a typical work week, I'm
in court from the afternoon to early evening, sometimes later depending
on the case.
Activity level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Sedentary (e.g. office job)
Stress level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 High stress (e.g. very high-paced work environment with
great responsibility)
How is your sleep rhythm and quality?
To be honest, pretty poor. During weeks with big cases, my mind races &
it's hard to fall asleep. I'm lucky if I'm asleep at 1 AM on most nights, but
At this point, you may choose to return this form to me to obtain a
price quote before filling in the rest. Please attach at least two full-body
pictures of yourself in clear lighting, preferably including a back double biceps
and front relaxed pose.

Legal disclaimer: By returning this intake form, you declare that while Menno
Henselmans is your personal trainer, all the substances you use are obtained
and used in accordance with their governing laws and you forego all rights to
hold Menno Henselmans liable for any damages caused in relation to following
his advice as your trainer.

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

I don't wake up too often (thankfully). On days I train, it's an early 6 AM
wake up call. Days I don't train, I can afford to sleep in until 8 AM or so.
How much caffeine do you consume daily on average or on a
typical work day?
My lifeblood; at least one Venti Americano from Starbucks heading into
work. There's always coffee available in the courthouse, so I'll have a cup
or two to keep me focused.
Equipment
Do you have skinfold calipers and if so, which one(s)?
No, but I'm willing to buy them. I have a measuring tape, which allows me
to measure neck:waist ratio. I also recently purchased a Skulpt Chisel on
the recommendation from a PT friend of mine, but I can't really figure out
how to work the damn thing…
What are the lightest barbell weight plates you have access to?
I train at Planet Fitness (sue me… pun intended). We have 5, 10, 25 & 45
lb plates.
What are the smallest weight increments between the sets of
dumbbells you have access to?
Dumbbells range from 5-80 lbs, by 5 lb increments. No barbells except
the short, "preassembled" barbells that range from 20-80 lbs in 10 lb
increments.
Do you have access to…
 a squat cage or rack? No… lots of Smith Machines though!
 a 45° hyperextension bench? Yes

BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

 a reverse hyper machine? No

 a glute-ham raise? No

 a standing calf raise machine? No
 a dip/chin-up belt? No, but I can buy one
 a leg curl machine and if so, is it seated, lying or standing? Seated
 a pair of knee wraps? No
 gymnastic rings? No
 a TRX or similar suspension device? No
 powerlifting bands (not the light home workout stuff)? No
 powerlifting chains? No

body up (see example image below) or different? No

Does your available equipment differ from that in most gyms in
any other way (e.g. you train in a Crossfit box or at home)?
Yeah, Planet Fitness isn't the greatest as you can see… but it's a block
away from the house & you can't beat the price.

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Supplements
Please list all the supplements you are currently taking:
- Vega Protein & Greens, always put it in my morning smoothie. I'll
have that after I train (I train fasted) or for breakfast.
- All that caffeine I talked about earlier
Genetics
 Wrist circumference (smallest point): 7.7 inches
 Ankle circumference (smallest point): 9.5 inches
 How much did you weigh before you started lifting and what was
your height at that point?: Same height (5'11"), ~270 lbs
 Were you heavy as a baby? Yes
 Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your right
hand is clearly visible. Ideally, place your right hand on a flat
surface with your fingers touching each other and then take a
picture of it. Ring finger is longer
Current Program
Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:
Don't currently follow a set program, with my schedule the way it is, I
basically eat when I can. My only objective is to keep it Vegan. Often, this
means just my morning smoothie with snacks throughout the day when
I'm in court (mostly fruit & nuts). Many times, I eat so little during the
day that I eat a few too many "Vegan Friendly" treats at night… and pasta
is my vice. I know a little about tracking macros, so I always try to get at
least the RDA of protein each day to cover my bases.
Please describe or attach your current training program in detail:
Currently train 3x a week, fasted in the morning before work. I have two
workouts, but I do the first one two times a week and the second one only
once. I'm keeping the focus on my upper body because I'm uneasy with
headings: Supplements
Genetics
picture of it. Ring finger is longer
Current Program

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

heavy leg work (and the Lunk Alarm). I also do 15 minutes of cardio after
every workout on the elliptical.

Workout 1 (Twice a Week)
Bench Press: 4 sets x 8 reps
Bicep Curls: 4 sets x 8 reps (last set is a drop set, I really feel the burn!)
Triceps Extensions: 4 sets x 8 reps (drop sets here, too)
Ab Crunches: 4 sets x 50 reps

Workout 2 (Once a Week)
Lat Pulldown Machine: 4 sets x 8 reps
Leg Press: 4 sets x 15 reps
Leg Curls: 4 sets x 15 reps
Leg Raises (for lower abs): 4 sets x 15 reps

Let's help our Vegan friend Markos get out of his rut. He's already lost a
substantial amount of weight, but many lifestyle factors are keeping him
from progressing any further.

Based on his markers (wrist, ankle, birth weight, digit ratio), his Genetic
Potential is actually above average, but his current strength level pegs
him as a Novice. His high levels of stress may compromise his ability to
recover, which is why many lifestyle modifications must be implemented
to sustain progress (discussed later).
Please return the filled in form to Coaching@BayesianBodybuilding.com
and attach at least two full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting
(without excessive shadows; not your most flattering Facebook selfies),
preferably including a back double biceps and front relaxed pose. If you
recently had blood work done, please attach its results as well.

BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

We can estimate that Markos' Anabolic Window length is roughly 36
hours; high amounts of physiological stress may actually prolong &
suppress the height of the rise in MPS post training, so let's say
somewhere around 40 hours. As a Novice by distinction, a good starting
point for frequency per body part would be training each twice per
week. Since he's willing to train 4 days per week, we can easily split this
into an Upper/Lower type split, with an off day splitting the 4 days.

As a Novice, 12 sets/body part/week will be a good starting point. Once
lifestyle modifications are made, his relatively high stress levels may be
offset by his better-than-average Genetic Potential. Since Markos is also
using arbitrary rep targets, we'll utilize Autoregulatory Volume Testing to
predict 1RMs & set working weights with 60% intensity for all exercises.

Cardio, direct ab work & dropsets will be ditched outright; once AV Testing
is complete, we can pair sets for efficiency. Volume must be spread more
efficiently than his current routine, which was very upper-body focused.

Vertical loading of the spine, as with traditional squats & deadlifts, are off
the table due to the injury in his lumbar spine. However, since he's
training at a Planet Fitness, he's somewhat limited in terms equipment
anyways. To work around the excessive amount of machines, lack of
barbells & his spinal injury, a higher amount of dumbbell work (especially
unilateral) will be utilized for his legs to allow him to progress with the
available equipment.

New Routine:

*Note: Front Delt volume won't be counted independently, as pressing/fly
movements generally cover the volume requirement for this muscle.
headings: point for frequency per body part would be training each twice per
week. Since he’s willing to train 4 days per week, we can easily split this

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Upper (6 sets/body part)
1A) Incline DB Bench: 3 x 60% - Chest, Triceps (1/2)
1B) Wide-Grip Pulldowns: 3 x 60% - Lats, Mid/Low Traps, Biceps, Rear
Delts
2A) Bayesian Flys: 3 x 60% - Chest, Front Delts
2B) Lat Prayers: 3 x 60% - Lats, Mid/Low Traps, Triceps (1/2), Rear Delts
3A) Seated Rope Face Pulls: 3 x 60% - Lat/Rear Delts, Upper/Mid Traps,
Bis (1/2)
3B) Cable Laterals: 3 x 60% - Lat Delt, Upper Traps
4A) Bayesian Curls: 3 x 60% - Biceps
4B) Unilateral Overhead Rope Ext: 3 x 60% - Triceps
Totals: Chest (6), Lats (6), Lat Delt (6), Rear Delt (6), Upper Trap (6),
Mid/Lower Traps (6), Biceps (6), Triceps (6)

Lower (6 sets/body part)
1A) Deficit Bulgarian Split Squats: 3 x 60% - Quads, Glutes, Calves
1B) Unilateral RDLs: 3 x 60% - Hams, Glutes
2A) Unilateral Leg Extensions: 3 x 60% - Quads
2B) Unilateral Leg Curls: 3 x 60% - Hams
2C) Leg Press Calve Jumps: 3 x 60% - Calves
Totals: Quads (6), Glutes (6), Hamstrings (6), Calves (6)

Progression should be linear once rep targets are established, at least for
the first few weeks/months. Undulating periodization should not be
needed.

In order to accommodate this level of volume, Markos needs to improve
his sleep, both in quantity & quality. An effective bed time should be 10
PM (if not earlier) to accommodate his 6 AM wake up time. This sleep
block will be implemented on all days. Light exposure manipulation,
meditation & melatonin supplementation would all be viable options to
explore. Caffeine intake (currently 700+ mg/day) would need to be

BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

substantially reduced, or eliminated completely for a 9-day period to re-
sensitize Markos to the positive effects. In either scenario, his caffeine
would be best saved for pre-workout since he's a morning trainee (7 AM),
and he should not exceed 250 mg.

As for the diet, Markos' job won't allow for the prototypical set meal times.
When he's in court, he'll be at the will of the case; this has two
implications:

1) The majority of energy should come around training in the AM
(which includes a meal pre training to avoid the fasted state) &
when he's home in the PM.
2) During his working hours, he'll have meal "windows" of about 2
hours to get smaller, snack-like meals in when he gets the chance
(i.e. during recess, jury deliberations, etc.). These "meals" (snacks,
really) will need to be pre-prepared & portable for quick
consumption. While it's not ideal, an additional serving of Vega
Protein can be consumed quickly & will bolster protein intake.

Speaking of protein, the current RDA of .8 g/kg (!!!) is woefully
inadequate, especially for a high-fiber, lower protein quality Vegan Diet.
Markos would be best served to even exceed 1.8 g/kg to ensure he's
getting not only enough protein, but a full spectrum of amino acids in
every meal. ~2.7 g/kg/day, towards the upper end of the range proposed
by Eric Helms in his protein debate with Menno, would be advisable in this
scenario to accommodate his diet preferences & growth potential.

On Training Days, this could look like:
Meal 1 - 6 AM (Pre Training), .7 g/kg protein, ~20% daily carbs/fat

BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Meal 2 - 9 AM (Post Training), .7 g/kg protein, ~40% daily carbs/fat
Meal 3 - 12 PM - 2 PM, .3 g/kg protein, trace carbs/fat
Meal 4 - 4 PM - 6 PM, .3 g/kg protein, trace carbs/fat
Meal 5 - 9 PM (Pre Bed), .7 g/kg protein, ~40% daily carbs/fat

Meal times should be maintained, even on non-training days. If training
Monday, Tuesday, Thursday & Friday, Sunday would be the only full day
outside of the Anabolic Window. A PSMF may be indicated, since the client
is targeting fat loss. A 40% deficit may be ideal based on his current body
comp & training status, but a 20-30% deficit may be more applicable due
to stress levels.

anyways. To work around the excessive amount of machines, lack of

unilateral) will be utilized for his legs to allow him to progress with the
available equipment.

New Routine:

*Note: Front Delt volume won't be counted independently, as pressing/fly
movements generally cover the volume requirement for this muscle.

Upper (6 sets/body part)

Delts
2A) Bayesian Flys: 3 x 60% - Chest, Front Delts
2B) Lat Prayers: 3 x 60% - Lats, Mid/Low Traps, Triceps (1/2), Rear Delts
3A) Seated Rope Face Pulls: 3 x 60% - Lat/Rear Delts, Upper/Mid Traps,
Bis (1/2)
3B) Cable Laterals: 3 x 60% - Lat Delt, Upper Traps
4A) Bayesian Curls: 3 x 60% - Biceps
4B) Unilateral Overhead Rope Ext: 3 x 60% - Triceps
Totals: Chest (6), Lats (6), Lat Delt (6), Rear Delt (6), Upper Trap (6),
Mid/Lower Traps (6), Biceps (6), Triceps (6)

Lower (6 sets/body part)
1A) Deficit Bulgarian Split Squats: 3 x 60% - Quads, Glutes, Calves
1B) Unilateral RDLs: 3 x 60% - Hams, Glutes
2A) Unilateral Leg Extensions: 3 x 60% - Quads
2B) Unilateral Leg Curls: 3 x 60% - Hams
2C) Leg Press Calve Jumps: 3 x 60% - Calves
Totals: Quads (6), Glutes (6), Hamstrings (6), Calves (6)

Progression should be linear once rep targets are established, at least for
the first few weeks/months. Undulating periodization should not be
needed.

In order to accommodate this level of volume, Markos needs to improve
his sleep, both in quantity & quality. An effective bed time should be 10
PM (if not earlier) to accommodate his 6 AM wake up time. This sleep
block will be implemented on all days. Light exposure manipulation,
meditation & melatonin supplementation would all be viable options to
explore. Caffeine intake (currently 700+ mg/day) would need to be


--- Page 10 ---
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

substantially reduced, or eliminated completely for a 9-day period to re-
sensitize Markos to the positive effects. In either scenario, his caffeine
would be best saved for pre-workout since he's a morning trainee (7 AM),
and he should not exceed 250 mg.

As for the diet, Markos' job won't allow for the prototypical set meal times.
When he's in court, he'll be at the will of the case; this has two
implications:

1) The majority of energy should come around training in the AM
(which includes a meal pre training to avoid the fasted state) &
when he's home in the PM.
2) During his working hours, he'll have meal "windows" of about 2
hours to get smaller, snack-like meals in when he gets the chance
(i.e. during recess, jury deliberations, etc.). These "meals" (snacks,
really) will need to be pre-prepared & portable for quick
consumption. While it's not ideal, an additional serving of Vega
Protein can be consumed quickly & will bolster protein intake.

Speaking of protein, the current RDA of .8 g/kg (!!!) is woefully
inadequate, especially for a high-fiber, lower protein quality Vegan Diet.
Markos would be best served to even exceed 1.8 g/kg to ensure he's
getting not only enough protein, but a full spectrum of amino acids in
every meal. ~2.7 g/kg/day, towards the upper end of the range proposed
by Eric Helms in his protein debate with Menno, would be advisable in this
scenario to accommodate his diet preferences & growth potential.

On Training Days, this could look like:
Meal 1 - 6 AM (Pre Training), .7 g/kg protein, ~20% daily carbs/fat


--- Page 11 ---
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Meal 2 - 9 AM (Post Training), .7 g/kg protein, ~40% daily carbs/fat
Meal 3 - 12 PM - 2 PM, .3 g/kg protein, trace carbs/fat
Meal 4 - 4 PM - 6 PM, .3 g/kg protein, trace carbs/fat
Meal 5 - 9 PM (Pre Bed), .7 g/kg protein, ~40% daily carbs/fat

Meal times should be maintained, even on non-training days. If training
Monday, Tuesday, Thursday & Friday, Sunday would be the only full day
outside of the Anabolic Window. A PSMF may be indicated, since the client
is targeting fat loss. A 40% deficit may be ideal based on his current body
comp & training status, but a 20-30% deficit may be more applicable due
to stress levels.
